headline: Michigan's ground game overwhelms UCLA in second-half defensive breakdown
body: Michigan's defense suffocated UCLA's rhythm in the second half, and the Wolverines' rushing attack—led by running back Brock's two touchdown performances—proved to be the difference in a 51-28 victory that looked closer on the scoreboard than it felt on the field. UCLA's Nico Iamaleava was brilliant in his own right, completing 30 of 46 passes for 368 yards and four touchdowns, but the Bruins' inability to protect the football and shore up their run defense allowed Michigan to control the line of scrimmage when it mattered most.

The turnover disparity told the story. UCLA coughed it up twice—both fumbles—while Michigan's ball security remained pristine. Those two UCLA miscues didn't just cost the Bruins field position; they disrupted the rhythm of an otherwise explosive passing offense. Iamaleava found receivers with consistency, and Harris's five catches for 86 yards gave UCLA vertical threats, but the offense was perpetually starting behind the chains after self-inflicted mistakes.

Michigan's 183 rushing yards against 111 for UCLA reflected a fundamental imbalance. Browne didn't need to shoulder the load; his 23 completions on 33 attempts for 336 yards and two touchdowns showcased efficiency over volume. The Wolverines' defense, anchored by Blackburn's seven tackles, forced UCLA into obvious passing situations and made the Bruins' high-volume passing attack less effective as the game wore on. For all of Iamaleava's talent on display, Michigan's complementary football in the trenches simply won out.
turningPoint: UCLA's second-half fumble in Michigan territory with the score within reach short-circuited any momentum the Bruins had built, allowing Michigan to widen the gap through consecutive scores where Brock punched in both of his touchdowns.

unsungHero.name: Brock
unsungHero.why: Browne gets credit for efficiency, but Brock's two rushing touchdowns and 80 yards on limited carries showed Michigan understood UCLA's defensive vulnerabilities and executed the game plan without fanfare—the definition of complementary football in a blowout win.
